cases.known_cause: FUNDAMENTAL (down): AMD issued a preliminary Q3 warning, cutting revenue guidance from ~$6.7B to ~$5.6B (a $1.1B miss), citing a weaker-than-expected PC market and significant inventory correction actions across the PC supply chain. Fundamental — the CEO directly disclosed that real PC demand was weaker than expected, materially lowering near-term revenue trajectory.

NON-FUNDAMENTAL: AMD rallied ~11% on no company-specific news. Nvidia reported blowout Q1 earnings the night before and AMD rose in sympathy as a perceived AI-chip beneficiary. Non-fundamental — nothing changed about AMD's business; the move was pure sector sympathy driven by Nvidia's earnings beat.

FUNDAMENTAL (up): AMD shares rose 9.9% to close at $128.37 — best day since May — the day after AMD launched the Instinct MI300X AI accelerator and disclosed Microsoft and Meta as named hyperscaler customers. Fundamental — named hyperscaler commitments converted a speculative AI roadmap into contracted data-center revenue and forced upward revisions to forward Instinct sales models.

NON-FUNDAMENTAL: AMD rose 10.7% to close at $181.86 the day after Nvidia reported blowout fiscal Q4 earnings. No AMD-specific news; the move was pure read-through optimism that AI capex strength would benefit AMD as the #2 GPU vendor. Non-fundamental — a near-replay of the May 2023 Nvidia-sympathy pattern, where AMD reacted to a competitor's earnings rather than to any change in AMD's contracts, products, or guidance.

FUNDAMENTAL: AMD climbed ~18% during the week with two stacked catalysts: (1) the Trump administration announced a short-term postponement of U.S. tariffs on Chinese electronics, removing a near-term cost-of-goods headwind on TSMC-fabricated chips; (2) AMD disclosed that Google Cloud's new C4D and H4D virtual machines are powered by 5th Gen AMD EPYC processors, with C4D delivering up to 80% higher throughput/vCPU and H4D optimized for HPC with Cloud RDMA scaling to tens of thousands of cores. Fundamental — both catalysts move forward cash flows: the tariff postponement removes a quantifiable cost headwind, and the Google Cloud C4D/H4D launch converts AMD's 5th Gen EPYC roadmap into deployed hyperscaler instances at one of the three largest cloud providers, expanding contracted server-CPU revenue.

FUNDAMENTAL (up): AMD and OpenAI announced a multi-year strategic partnership; OpenAI committed to deploying AMD Instinct MI450 GPUs. Fundamental — AMD landed OpenAI as a committed customer for its next-gen AI GPU, which directly implies future data center revenue that wasn't in analyst models the day before.

FUNDAMENTAL (down): AMD issued a preliminary Q3 warning, cutting revenue guidance from ~$6.7B to ~$5.6B (a $1.1B miss), citing a weaker-than-expected PC market and significant inventory correction actions across the PC supply chain. Non-fundamental — a near-replay of the May 2023 Nvidia-sympathy pattern, where AMD reacted to a competitor's earnings rather than to any change in AMD's contracts, products, or guidance.

unscored_explanation: Fixtures only carry ground truth for the fundamental-vs-non strategy. Other strategies are computed and surfaced for comparison but cannot be scored against ground truth until fixtures add the matching expected fields (predicted move size, per-dimension persistence, etc.).
